title: Common Misconceptions About Work Breakdown Structures (WBS)
summary: Clarifies the most frequent misunderstandings about Work Breakdown Structures, explaining what a WBS does and does not include.
content: Misconception 1: The WBS is a schedule – false; it contains no timing. Misconception 2: The WBS includes cost estimates – false; budgeting follows the WBS. Misconception 3: Activities can be listed in the WBS – false; activities belong to the schedule. Misconception 4: A WBS must be fully detailed before any schedule work – partially true; a high‑level WBS can support a ROM schedule, but detailed decomposition is needed for accurate planning.
can_answer: Is a WBS a budgeting tool?
Can a WBS be used as a project schedule?
Do activities belong in a WBS?
Must a WBS be fully detailed before creating a schedule?
